description: Merck & Co. is a global pharmaceutical company whose immunotherapy blockbuster Keytruda has become one of the best-selling drugs in history, while its HPV vaccine Gardasil, animal health division, and a broadening oncology and cardiometabolic pipeline round out a portfolio designed to outlast the patent cliff everyone on Wall Street is already dreading.

overview.title: Company at a Glance
paragraphs: Merck & Co., Inc. (ticker: MRK) is a Rahway, New Jersey-based multinational pharmaceutical company operating across human health and animal health segments. Founded in 1891 as a U.S. subsidiary of the German Merck KGaA, Merck became fully independent following World War I and has since grown into one of the ten largest pharmaceutical companies by revenue globally.

The company's human health segment—responsible for roughly 90% of revenue—develops, manufactures, and markets prescription medicines, vaccines, and biologics across oncology, infectious diseases, cardiometabolic disorders, and immunology. Its animal health segment, Merck Animal Health, is among the world's largest producers of veterinary pharmaceuticals and vaccines, serving companion and livestock markets in over 140 countries.

overview.title: Keytruda: The Drug That Ate the Portfolio
paragraphs: Pembrolizumab, sold as Keytruda, is Merck's PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor and the world's top-selling oncology drug. Approved across more than 40 cancer indications—including non-small cell lung cancer, melanoma, head and neck cancers, and triple-negative breast cancer—Keytruda generated approximately $25 billion in revenue in 2024, representing over 40% of the company's total sales. It is, without exaggeration, the financial engine of modern Merck.

Keytruda's dominance has also made it a strategic liability. Core composition-of-matter patents expire around 2028, and biosimilar entry in the U.S. is expected by the early 2030s. Merck is pursuing a subcutaneous formulation of Keytruda (co-developed with Alteogen) and exploring fixed-dose combinations to extend commercial exclusivity and patient convenience—strategies that buy time while the pipeline is meant to provide the longer-term answer.

overview.title: Gardasil and the Vaccines Franchise
paragraphs: Gardasil (and Gardasil 9) is Merck's recombinant HPV vaccine and one of the most consequential public health products of the past two decades, with demonstrated efficacy in preventing cervical, oropharyngeal, and other HPV-associated cancers. Global sales peaked above $8 billion annually, driven by robust U.S. uptake and a major expansion into China through local distributor Zhifei Biological.

China became both Gardasil's largest growth market and its most acute vulnerability. Beginning in 2023, Chinese sales collapsed as Zhifei drew down excess inventory and national demand softened amid economic uncertainty and public skepticism. Merck disclosed a material reduction in Gardasil shipments to China through 2024–2025, resetting expectations for the franchise. Recovery timelines remain uncertain, making Gardasil a closely watched indicator for the stock.

overview.title: Pipeline and Acquisitions Strategy
paragraphs: Merck has used acquisitions to fill the post-Keytruda pipeline gap. Notable transactions include the $11.5 billion acquisition of Prometheus Biosciences (2023, for tulisokibart in inflammatory bowel disease), the $1.3 billion acquisition of Harpoon Therapeutics (2023, T-cell engagers), and the $8.9 billion acquisition of Acceleron Pharma (2021, yielding sotatercept for pulmonary arterial hypertension, marketed as Winrevair). Islatravir (HIV prevention), MK-0616 (oral PCSK9 inhibitor for LDL cholesterol), and a growing suite of oncology bispecifics and ADCs round out the mid-stage pipeline.

The company has also deepened its investment in mRNA vaccine technology through a collaboration with Moderna on a personalized cancer vaccine (mRNA-4157/V940) for adjuvant melanoma, reflecting a broader industry pivot toward individualized immunotherapy. Merck's pipeline is larger and more diversified than it was five years ago, but investors are watching carefully to see whether it is large enough to offset Keytruda's inevitable erosion.

overview.title: Competitive Position and Key Risks
paragraphs: Merck competes against Bristol Myers Squibb (Opdivo), Roche (Tecentriq), AstraZeneca, Pfizer, Johnson & Johnson, and a growing cohort of Chinese biopharmaceutical firms in its core oncology markets. In vaccines, it competes with GSK and Pfizer. The company's scale, manufacturing capabilities, and established relationships with oncology prescribers provide durable competitive advantages, though the concentrated revenue profile around Keytruda remains the primary investor concern.

Additional risks include the Inflation Reduction Act's Medicare drug price negotiation provisions—Keytruda is subject to negotiated pricing beginning in 2026—regulatory scrutiny of Gardasil in international markets, integration execution risk from its acquisition spree, and broader macroeconomic sensitivity in emerging markets. Merck has also faced litigation over the mumps component of its M-M-R II vaccine, adding headline risk to an otherwise strong franchise.

sourceSuggestions.title: Merck Investor Relations – Annual Reports and SEC Filings
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary source for audited financials, segment revenue breakdowns, and management commentary on pipeline milestones and Keytruda trajectory.

sourceSuggestions.title: Merck Pipeline – Official Pipeline Page
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Authoritative list of clinical-stage compounds by phase and indication; essential for tracking post-Keytruda portfolio development.

sourceSuggestions.title: SEC EDGAR – Merck & Co. 10-K and 10-Q Filings
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Unfiltered regulatory disclosures including litigation risks, segment financials, and forward-looking risk factors not always highlighted in earnings presentations.

sourceSuggestions.title: Companies Market Cap – Merck Market Capitalization History
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Clean historical market cap data useful for contextualizing Merck's valuation relative to peers and against Keytruda revenue milestones.

sourceSuggestions.title: FDA Drug Approvals Database – Keytruda Approval History
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Tracks all U.S. label expansions for Keytruda, providing a timeline of regulatory milestones that correlate with revenue inflection points.

sourceSuggestions.title: Evaluate Pharma / Evaluate Vantage – Merck Coverage
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Industry-respected analysis of pipeline valuations, patent cliff modeling, and competitive dynamics in oncology immunotherapy.

sourceSuggestions.title: STAT News – Merck & Co. Coverage
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: High-quality science and business journalism covering Merck's clinical trial results, acquisition rationale, and drug pricing controversies.

sourceSuggestions.title: Merck 2024 Full-Year Earnings Press Release
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Most recent audited annual results with segment-level Keytruda and Gardasil performance, guidance commentary, and pipeline update.
